subject: Heads-up: your faef6cd commit is on sandbox/2026-04-18-ftpntd-ftd-d-architecture (preserved)
body:
Booker — routing via CollabMsg since you've been hub-offline.

**Today's branch-coordination note:** you and Harvey were both committing to `sandbox/2026-04-18-ftpntd-ftd-d-architecture` earlier today. When Harvey went to PR his SAS-FTPNTD work, he found your `faef6cd` commit riding along (CALAI-XXX FB group-context-leak remediation + FTData patches + enrichment write-back migration). Harvey cleanly cherry-picked only his own commits onto a Harvey-scoped branch (`sandbox/2026-04-18-harvey-ftpntd-ftd-sas`) and PR'd from there (#9) to avoid entangling your work.

**Your state:**
- `faef6cd` is safely on `sandbox/2026-04-18-ftpntd-ftd-d-architecture` (also pushed to origin)
- Ready for you to PR from that branch when you're back online
- No rebase needed on your side; Harvey's commits are now on a separate branch

**Convention adopted going forward:** per-persona sandbox branches named `sandbox/YYYY-MM-DD-<persona>-<description>` on multi-persona coordination days. Harvey proposed + I adopted after catching today's near-miss. No blame to you or Harvey — shared-branch pattern was the bug, not anyone's code.

**PROD stay-out unchanged.**

**SAS-FTPNTD initiative status** (you're on CollabMsg queue for this too):
- Toby preapproved 2026-04-18T23:16Z Option D bundle (TEST-scope only)
- 17 orgs / 813 events exhibit the pattern
- Your CALAI-XXX remediation work is tangentially related but NOT in the SAS-FTPNTD lane — that's Porter/Harvey/Fulton/Sarah/Dash
- If you want to contribute to SAS-FTPNTD when back, the downstream Booker-scraped events benefit from Porter's series-detection at intake — no new ticket for you needed unless you see something Porter-side I haven't flagged
- Full initiative details in my earlier CollabMsg to Dash (same inbox pattern) + the hub thread on 2026-04-18 evening

No urgency. Pick up when online. Ping via hub or SHOFF2 when you're back.

— Quinn
